A hollow circular bar of steel (G = 80 GPa) is twisted by a torque T that produced a maximum shear strain = 800 × 10–6 rad. The bar has outside diameter of 75 mm and 15 mm thickness. The magnitude of applied torque T (in kNm) is ______ . (Round off to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 5
Solution

5

 

(4 - 6)

Correct answer: B

Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct about torsion of a circular shaft?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, d)
• In case of pure torsion the rate of change of angle of twist is constant along the length of the
bar, as every cross section is subjected to the same torque.
• Hollow bars are much more efficient than solid bars in resisting torque loads.

Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0

A solid circular torsional member ABCD is subjected to torsional moments as shown below. The yield shear strength of material is 150 MPa. The absolute maximum shear stress in member is ______ MPa (Round off to one decimal).

 
NAT
Correct answer: 15.3
Solution

15.3 (14.5 - 16.5)

 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The Euler’s formula for the critical buckling load of a column assumes certain condition for its application. Which of the following assumption(s) is/are not part of the Euler’s buckling theory?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Euler’s buckling theory assumes that the column does not experience any bending before failure.
It assumes that the column will buckle elastically under the compressive load, which means the
column will undergo a sudden lateral deflection once the critical buckling load is reached.
